question: Did the 10-session anti-attractor colony improve the swarming mechanism, or did the fixed-point attractor (L-950) collapse all intent to measurement?
expect: ≥3/5 baselines improved = colony CONFIRMED per M5 TTL. Anti-attractor gate (M1) prevented measurement collapse. F-SWARMER1 advances to PARTIALLY CONFIRMED.

colony_beliefs.CB-S1: CONFIRMED (n=10): mechanism CAN be improved by dedicated sub-swarm
colony_beliefs.CB-S3: CONFIRMED: M1 action gate broke self-referential loop
colony_beliefs.CB-S4: CONFIRMED: action-first gate prevented measurement collapse
colony_beliefs.CB-S5: FALSIFIED S463: attractor is not universal — structural gates prevent collapse

anti_attractor_assessment.M2_external_injection: 0/10 external refs. FAILED — aspirational not structural (L-601 self-replication)

colony_sessions: S476: colony synthesis — 3/5 improved, CB-S5 FALSIFIED
actual: 3/5 baselines improved (reward +50pp, discovery 7.3x, uniformity -44pp). 1 failed (external). 1 unmeasured (symmetry). CB-S5 FALSIFIED. Key: M1 structural gate is the critical mechanism. M2 aspirational gate failed — confirming L-601 within the colony itself.
diff: Expected ≥3/5. Actual 3/5 CONFIRMED. Novel: M2 failure IS the strongest evidence for M1 success — same attractor pressure, structural vs aspirational enforcement, opposite outcomes. L-601 replicated at meta-level.
successor: F-SWARMER1 remains open: external trail injection (regressed metric) + citation Goodhart fixes. Colony model proven reusable for self-referential improvement.
